description: Strong senior evaluated against University of Washington (KB acceptance ~39.15%, SELECTIVE per fit_computation.py's tier bucketing — 25-40% band). No floor applies; the ceiling rule says the school CANNOT be REACH or SUPER_REACH (must be SAFETY or TARGET) but allows either above that.
business_rationale: SELECTIVE schools are the bread-and-butter target tier for many strong students — flagship publics, mid-T50 privates. Operators rely on the algorithm to distinguish a TARGET (achievable with effort) from a SAFETY (achievable confidently). A regression that misclassified a 39% school as REACH would surface immediately via the ceiling invariant in this archetype.
tests: SELECTIVE band (25-40% acceptance): selectivity ceiling forces SAFETY or TARGET; cannot be REACH or SUPER_REACH
All four factors present with bounded scores
Selectivity factor in {0} per the 25-40% rubric (no penalty, no bonus)
All 8 advisory blocks present and non-empty
